## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Spec-driven mechanical refactor. ANCHOR's value shape changes from
  content-addressed identifier to domain-blind locator. No new
  capabilities; existing flows preserved.
  
  ## Goals
  
  Redefine `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` as a `package-path:tag` locator
  matching the wrest/jettison domain-blind locator pattern in RBS0.
  Today ANCHOR is `<sanitized-origin>-<10-hex>` implicitly under
  `enshrines/`; new shape carries its namespace explicitly so it can
  name an enshrinement OR (in ₢BBAA0) a hallmark.
  
  ## Wrap criterion
  
  - Spec sweep: RBS0 RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR definition, RBSAE enshrine,
    RBSAC conjure preflight + resolve, plus any consumers using the
    short-form anchor.
  - Code consumers updated: enshrine writeback, conjure resolution,
    validation patterns.
  - Existing populated rbrv.env values migrated to locator shape.
  - Fast suite green; previously-green onboarding-sequence cases
    still pass.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Hallmark-base support — ₢BBAA0.
  - Airgap chain integration — ₢BBAAs verifies.


Recommended: `jjx_arm` ₢₢BBAAz or mount BB

c547c62a  ₢BBAAs    Narrow the prior commit's enforce-skip list to drop rbob_ordain. The split that made my prior arm too wide: ordain has TWO entry points and they live in different CLIs. rbw-fO → rbfd_ordain is vessel-keyed (foundry-direct), goes through rbfd_cli.sh, never loads a nameplate, never runs zrbrn_enforce — and it is the entry point onboarding-sequence actually invokes (RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ORDAIN = "rbw-fO" at rbtdrm_manifest.rs:30). rbw-tO → rbob_ordain is nameplate-keyed (Theurge OrdainCycle, imprint channel), goes through rbob_cli.sh, IS the gate where zrbrn_enforce earns its keep — it is the natural pre-cloud-spend regime-validity check on a nameplate-affiliated build. Putting rbob_ordain into the skip list achieved nothing for the immediate failure (the failing path is rbw-cKS → rbob_kludge_sentry, not any rbob_ordain) and silently weakened the rbw-tO validation surface. Final skip list: rbob_kludge, rbob_kludge_bottle, rbob_kludge_sentry — all three are nameplate-keyed kludge entry points that write RBRN_*_HALLMARK and self-veto on marshal-zero blank-by-design state. rbob_kludge stays in the list because it is reachable two ways: directly via rbw-tK (Theurge KludgeCycle) and indirectly via rbob_kludge_bottle's body (rbob_bottle.sh:481-484 is just zrbob_sentinel; rbob_kludge); ${z_command} is set in furnish from the zipper-registered name for the dispatched tabtarget, so all three matter. The differential foundry-kindle block at the tail of zrbob_furnish keeps rbob_ordain — ordain still needs zrbfd_kindle to perform the Cloud Build; only the enforce dispatch drops it. Build syntax-check green (bash -n); 91/91 rbtd unit tests pass; fast suite 25/26 (pre-existing rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels unchanged from BBAAs; regime-smoke skipped under suite-level fail-fast as expected). Empirical onboarding-sequence re-run pending operator go-ahead per docket discipline.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
142cf920  ₢BBAAs    Fix kludge/ordain self-veto on RBRN_*_HALLMARK surfaced by onboarding-sequence empirical run. Case 2 kludge-tadmor failed at the kludge-sentry sub-step with 'RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK must not be empty' — zrbrn_enforce ran unconditionally in rbob_cli.sh after zrbrn_kindle, but rbob_kludge_sentry / rbob_kludge / rbob_kludge_bottle / rbob_ordain are precisely the commands that write RBRN_*_HALLMARK; their kindle path self-vetoed on the very fields they were about to populate. Marshal-zero blanks both hallmarks by design (rblm_cli.sh:168), and rbtdrp_pristine.rs:117 explicitly catalogs them as RBTDRP_RBRN_BLANK_FIELDS — pristine state IS blank-hallmark by contract. Mirrors the yoke/RBRV self-veto pattern fixed in BBAAs:4097610f, but applied in-CLI rather than via file split: rbob_cli handles a coherent multi-command surface (kludge/ordain write hallmarks; charge/start/stop/hail/rack/scry/info/validate/charged require populated hallmarks for ZRBOB_*_IMAGE refs), so splitting like rbfl_cli vs rbrv_cli would fragment the surface. Survey of in-codebase patterns: Pattern A (file split, rbfl_cli/rbrv_cli) — not applicable. Pattern B (command-keyed differential within one CLI, already used at rbob_cli.sh:164-168 for differential zrbfd_kindle) — applied. Pattern C (rbfd_enshrine internal vessel resolution via zrbfc_resolve_vessel/zrbfc_load_vessel) — deliberately avoided per operator note that it was 'messy' in the prior outing. Replaced the unconditional zrbrn_enforce at line 152 with a case on ${z_command} parallel to the existing foundry-kindle dispatch — the four write-target commands skip enforce, all others enforce as today. Strict steady-state validator (rbw-rnv via rbrn_cli.sh) untouched — remains the path for explicit 'is this regime steady-state valid?' questions. Build syntax-check green (bash -n); 91/91 rbtd unit tests pass; fast suite 25/26 (pre-existing rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels unchanged from BBAAs; regime-smoke skipped under suite-level fail-fast as expected). Empirical onboarding-sequence re-run pending operator go-ahead per docket discipline.                                                                                                                                                                                           
742f5116  ₢BBAAs    Fix onboarding-sequence enshrine API mismatch surfaced by empirical run. rbfd_enshrine is per-vessel (requires sigil arg); the BBAAu buildout's rbtdro_enshrine invoked rbw-dE with no args expecting a sweep semantics that does not exist — case 2 enshrine-bases failed at start with 'Vessel argument required'. Survey of onboarding handbook tracks (rbhodf, rbhoda, rbhofc) confirmed: enshrine corresponds to no per-vessel sweep workflow — it appears only inside the airgap chain (rbhoda) twice (upstream rust base before forge; forge hallmark before airgap-bottle). Sentry-tether (debian:bookworm-slim) is built tethered conjure with no enshrine; ccyolo (node:22-slim) is built via local kludge with no Cloud Build; jupyter is conjure-tethered. Three independent upstream bases, no sharing. Refactor: (1) delete case 2 enshrine-bases entirely — function pair, section registration, file-header doc, test assertions; case count drops 9→8. (2) rbtdro_enshrine takes a vessel_sigil parameter and forwards it to rbw-dE as the folio. (3) ordain-airgap restructured to commit between sub-operations to satisfy ordain's clean-tree precondition: enshrine-upstream(forge sigil) + commit, ordain-forge, enshrine-forge(airgap sigil) + commit, ordain-airgap + propagate-moriah + commit (three commits within one case, mirroring the kludge_nameplate pattern). Commit message strings re-prefixed BBAAs (was BBAAu) for current-pace consistency. Also: jupyter rbrv.env was missing RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN entirely — added python:3.12.7-slim-bookworm (sourced verbatim from the vessel's own Dockerfile FROM line, unchanged since vessel creation in commit 635f87e5); blank RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR= appended after # eof mirroring the sentry-tether layout convention. Cleanup pass: dropped all '// ── Case N: name ──' section header bands within rbtdro_onboarding.rs — hardcoded numbers rot on reorder and the function name + /// doc immediately below renders the band redundant; reworked file-header to point at the cases array as source of truth instead of duplicating an enumerated list. Build green; 91/91 unit tests pass; tt/rbtd-s.FixtureCase.onboarding-sequence.sh emits 8 cases in declared order.   - Spec sweep: RBS0 RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR definition, RBSAE enshrine,
    RBSAC conjure preflight + resolve, plus any consumers using the
    short-form anchor.
  - Code consumers updated: enshrine writeback, conjure resolution,
    validation patterns.
  - Existing populated rbrv.env values migrated to locator shape.
  - Fast suite green; previously-green onboarding-sequence cases
    still pass.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Hallmark-base support — ₢BBAA0.
  - Airgap chain integration — ₢BBAAs verifies.


Recommended: `jjx_arm` ₢₢BBAAz or mount BB

content.content:
1	## Context
2	
3	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
4	
5	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
6	
7	## Single operator prerequisite chain
8	
9	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
10	
11	## Entry contract — load-bearing
12	
13	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
14	
15	- Working tree clean
16	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
17	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
18	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
19	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
20	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
21	
22	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
23	
24	## Failure mode contract
25	
26	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
27	
28	## Release-branch execution contract
29	
30	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
31	
32	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
33	
34	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
35	
36	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
37	
38	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
39	
40	## Tier layering
41	
42	Three tiers, escalating cost:
43	
44	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
45	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
46	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
47	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
48	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
49	
50	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
51	
52	## Gauntlet test suite — design
53	
54	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
55	
56	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
57	
58	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
59	
60	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
61	
62	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
63	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
64	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
65	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
66	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
67	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
68	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
69	
70	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
71	
72	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
73	
74	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
75	
76	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
77	
78	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
79	
80	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
81	
82	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
83	
84	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
85	
86	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
87	
88	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
89	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
90	
91	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
92	
93	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
94	
95	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
96	
97	## Suite-level fail-fast
98	
99	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
100	
101	## No-teardown decision
102	
103	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
104	
105	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
106	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
107	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
108	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
109	
110	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
111	
112	## Operator scope
113	
114	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
115	
116	## Coupling to ₣A_
117	
118	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
119	
120	## Locked design decisions
121	
122	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
123	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
124	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
125	
126	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
127	
128	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
129	
130	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
131	
132	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
133	
134	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
135	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
136	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
137	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
138	
139	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
140	
141	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
142	
143	## References
144	
145	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
146	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
147	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
148	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
149	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

file.content:
## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

content.text:
Mounted ₣BB.

## Heat: rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (29/43 done, racing)

The release-qualification heat builds `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero state was just committed. Recent BBAAs activity has been resolving onboarding-sequence empirical-run failures (kludge/ordain self-veto, enshrine API mismatch, plantuml fan-out, jupyter rbrv.env hole) — those fix commits are pending operator empirical re-run.

**Other active racing heats**: ₣BA cleanups (2/9), ₣A_ depot-regen (13/20), ₣A- windows-procedures (8/21), ₣A6 handbook-restart (46/55), ₣Ah jjk-school-breeze (8/19), ₣AU release-finalize (38/44).

## Next pace: rbrv-anchor-locator-format (₢BBAAz) [rough]

**Docket**: spec-driven mechanical refactor. `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` value shape changes from content-addressed `<sanitized-origin>-<10-hex>` (implicitly under `enshrines/`) to a `package-path:tag` locator matching the wrest/jettison domain-blind pattern in RBS0. New shape carries its namespace explicitly so it can name an enshrinement OR a hallmark (the latter being ₢BBAA0). Out of scope here: hallmark-base support and airgap-chain integration (₢BBAAs verifies).

**Wrap criterion**: spec sweep (RBS0 + RBSAE enshrine + RBSAC conjure), code consumers (enshrine writeback, conjure resolution, validation), populated rbrv.env values migrated, fast suite green, onboarding-sequence cases still pass.

## Silks assessment

`rbrv-anchor-locator-format` reads true to the docket — locator is the shape, format is the operation. No rename suggested.

## Proposed approach

- **Spec-first read**: RBS0 `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` definition, RBSAE enshrine writeback, RBSAC conjure preflight/resolve — establish what the new locator shape looks like in spec voice before touching code
- **Consumer enumeration**: grep `RBRV_IMAGE.*ANCHOR` across `Tools/rbk/` for writeback/resolve/validate sites; any short-form parsers (the `<10-hex>` regex)
- **Code edits + rbrv.env migration** in lockstep — code accepts new shape, populated env files migrate to it, both land in same notch
- **Fast suite** + spot-check onboarding-sequence cases that were green before BBAAs fixes

## Execution strategy

Sequential, **opus**: spec-driven refactor has judgment surface (locator grammar choice, validation regex shape) that doesn't decompose cleanly. Single-thread thinking — spec choice constrains code shape, code shape constrains migration.

Ready to proceed? Or would you prefer I start with the spec read first and surface the new locator grammar for your review before touching consumers?

Format: `«sanitized-origin»-«10-char-sha256»` (e.g., `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`).
The anchor serves as both the path segment and the tag on the enshrined package, and also as the regime variable value.
Enshrined images are stored as peer packages under the shared `enshrines/` namespace (`«prefix»enshrines/«anchor»:«anchor»`), not per-vessel — vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined artifact.
When present, conjure resolves the base image from GAR via this anchor.
When absent, conjure passes
{rbrv_image_origin}
through as the upstream tag (development path).

[[rbrv_group_grafting]]
//axvr_group axd_conditional
{rbrv_group_grafting}::
Variables for grafting vessels within
{rbrv_regime}.

[[rbrv_graft_image]]
//axvr_variable axd_conditional rbst_image_ref
{rbrv_graft_image}::
Default local image reference for
{rbrv_regime}
grafting vessels.
Optional; when empty, the image reference must be provided as a runtime parameter to
{rbtgo_ark_graft}.

[[rbrv_graft_optional_dockerfile]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional rbst_path
{rbrv_graft_optional_dockerfile}::
Optional path to Dockerfile used for the local build in
{rbrv_regime}
grafting vessels.
When configured, the Dockerfile content is embedded in the
{rbtga_ark_about}
artifact as `recipe.txt`.

[[rbrn_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_manifold
{rbrn_regime}::
Configuration regime for crucible deployment.
RBRN assignment files in `.rbk/{moniker}/rbrn.env` define container deployment and network security settings.

[[rbrn_moniker]]
//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname

Unique moniker for the crucible instance; 2-12 characters.

[[rbrn_description]]

Human-readable description of the service purpose; 0-120 characters.

[[rbrn_runtime]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axt_enumeration
{rbrn_runtime}::
Container runtime for service deployment.

[[rbrn_runtime_docker]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value
{rbrn_runtime_docker}::
Use Docker container runtime.

[[rbrn_runtime_podman]]
//axl_voices axt_enum_value
{rbrn_runtime_podman}::
Use Podman container runtime.

[[rbst_xname]]

{rbst_xname}::
Cross-context safe identifier, 1-64 characters.
Forms basis for directory names, monikers, and artifact naming.

[[rbst_description]]
//axl_voices axtu_string
{rbst_description}::
Human-readable documentation text.
0-240 characters; used for identification in logs, messages, and documentation contexts.

[[rbst_image_ref]]
//axl_voices axtu_string
{rbst_image_ref}::
Fully-qualified container image reference.
1-256 characters; format: `registry/namespace/image:tag` (e.g., `docker.io/library/nginx:1.27-alpine`).

[[rbst_locator]]
//axl_voices axtu_string
{rbst_locator}::
Repository-local artifact identifier combining a package path and tag.
Format: `package-path:tag` where `package-path` is a relative path under the categorical root (e.g., `hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/image`, `reliquaries/«STAMP»/syft`, `enshrines/«ANCHOR»`).
Identifies a single artifact within the
{rbtgi_repository}
without repeating registry host or path.
Domain-blind: the package path embedded in the locator is sufficient to dispatch a delete or pull against any of the three categorical domains (hallmarks, reliquaries, enshrinements).

[[rbst_hallmark]]
//axl_voices axtu_string
{rbst_hallmark}::
Dual-timestamp {rbtga_hallmark} identifying a specific build of a vessel.
Format: `«MODE»YYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS` where `«MODE»` is `c` (conjure), `b` (bind), or `g` (graft).
Example: `c260224153022-r260224160530`; 1-128 characters.
Regex: `[cbg]\d{12}-r\d{12}`.

[[rbst_reliquary_stamp]]
//axl_voices axtu_string
{rbst_reliquary_stamp}::
Datestamp identifying a reliquary — the GAR namespace holding a coherent set of mirrored tool images for builder use.
Format: `r` + `YYMMDDHHMMSS` (e.g., `r260327172456`); 13 characters.
Regex: `r\d{12}`.
Mints once at {rbtgo_depot_inscribe} time and serves both as the GAR path segment (`reliquaries/«STAMP»/...`) and the per-tool tag, so a single value identifies the namespace and pins every member.

[[rbst_path]]
//axl_voices axtu_path
{rbst_path}::
Repository-context path, typically constructed from regime variables.

[[rbst_platform_list]]
//axl_voices axtu_string axd_list
{rbst_platform_list}::
Space-delimited list of container platform identifiers.
Each element follows `os/arch[/variant]` format (e.g., `linux/amd64 linux/arm64 linux/arm/v7`).

{rbst_binfmt_policy}::

4:NOTE: This operation enshrines upstream base images into
6:as content-addressed, human-readable anchors.
11:from its upstream registry to GAR with an anchor tag using
14:The anchor results are returned via `buildStepOutputs` and
15:{rbrv_image_anchor}
21:when anchored base images are desired.
22:Conjure works without anchors (passing ORIGIN through), but anchored images provide reproducibility and air-gap readiness.
28:The enshrine operation:
49:as the builder image (script: `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`)
50:... `_RBGE_*` substitutions: `_RBGE_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGE_GAR_PATH`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` (n = 1, 2, 3; empty for unused slots). Base images are stored in the shared `enshrines/` namespace, with each anchor as a peer package (`«PREFIX»enshrines/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»`), not per-vessel.
54:... Fetches the raw upstream manifest (`skopeo inspect --raw`) and computes its sha256 digest (`sha256sum` of the raw manifest bytes)
56:`<<DIGEST>>` -- the sha256 digest of the upstream manifest (manifest list digest for multi-platform images, single manifest digest for single-platform images)
57:... Constructs anchor: sanitize full origin string (`:` `/` replaced with `-`), append first 10 hex chars of sha256 digest
58:... Example: `python:3.11-slim` with `sha256:abc123def4...` produces `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`
59:... Copies upstream image into the shared `enshrines/` namespace in GAR as a peer package at `«PREFIX»enshrines/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` using `skopeo copy --all`, transferring the manifest list and all per-platform manifests atomically. Each anchor is its own package under the namespace — the path segment carries the anchor identity, and the tag redundantly restates it so divergence is a visible bug. The anchor is vessel-independent — multiple vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined package.
61:copy succeeds -- the anchor tag is now a permanent, content-addressed reference in
65:.. Writes JSON anchor results to `/builder/outputs/output` (returned as `buildStepOutputs`)
71:... Write anchor string to `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` in the vessel's `rbrv.env`
74:{rbrv_image_anchor}
75:Per-slot anchor string written to the vessel regime.
78:Successful execution produces anchored base image references in
81:{rbrv_image_anchor}
86:Multi-platform conjure vessels pull the correct platform from the anchored manifest list automatically.
87:Re-running enshrine with the same
89:but a different upstream digest produces a new anchor (upstream image was updated).

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation enshrines upstream base images into
5	{gar_service}
6	as content-addressed, human-readable anchors.
7	The
8	{rbtr_director}
9	submits a Cloud Build job that copies each declared
10	{rbrv_image_origin}
11	from its upstream registry to GAR with an anchor tag using
12	the reliquary skopeo image
13	(`skopeo copy --all`), preserving the manifest list and all per-platform manifests for multi-platform images.
14	The anchor results are returned via `buildStepOutputs` and
15	{rbrv_image_anchor}
16	is then written back to the vessel regime locally.
17	
18	NOTE: Enshrine is a pre-build preparation step.
19	It must be run before
20	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
21	when anchored base images are desired.
22	Conjure works without anchors (passing ORIGIN through), but anchored images provide reproducibility and air-gap readiness.
23	
24	NOTE: Enshrine runs on Cloud Build, not the Director's workstation.
25	No local skopeo installation is required.
26	The GCB step uses Mason SA ambient credentials via the metadata server for GAR writes.
27	
28	The enshrine operation:
29	
30	//axhopt_typed_parameter
31	{rbrv_sigil}
32	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
33	.. {rbbc_require}
34	vessel provided as first argument
35	.. {rbbc_require}
36	vessel contains at least one
37	{rbrv_image_origin}
38	declaration (slots 1-3)
39	.. {rbbc_require}
40	vessel mode is `conjure` (`RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`)
41	.. {rbbc_require}
42	reliquary tool images exist in GAR (HEAD check on `docker:latest` canary)
43	
44	//axhos_step
45	. *Submit Enshrine Cloud Build Job*:
46	.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
47	... Single step using
48	the reliquary skopeo image
49	as the builder image (script: `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`)
50	... `_RBGE_*` substitutions: `_RBGE_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGE_GAR_PATH`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` (n = 1, 2, 3; empty for unused slots). Base images are stored in the shared `enshrines/` namespace, with each anchor as a peer package (`«PREFIX»enshrines/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»`), not per-vessel.
51	... Mason SA, private pool, timeout
52	.. Submit via `builds.create` and wait for completion
53	.. The GCB step, for each non-empty ORIGIN slot:
54	... Fetches the raw upstream manifest (`skopeo inspect --raw`) and computes its sha256 digest (`sha256sum` of the raw manifest bytes)
55	... {rbbc_store}
56	`<<DIGEST>>` -- the sha256 digest of the upstream manifest (manifest list digest for multi-platform images, single manifest digest for single-platform images)
57	... Constructs anchor: sanitize full origin string (`:` `/` replaced with `-`), append first 10 hex chars of sha256 digest
58	... Example: `python:3.11-slim` with `sha256:abc123def4...` produces `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`
59	... Copies upstream image into the shared `enshrines/` namespace in GAR as a peer package at `«PREFIX»enshrines/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` using `skopeo copy --all`, transferring the manifest list and all per-platform manifests atomically. Each anchor is its own package under the namespace — the path segment carries the anchor identity, and the tag redundantly restates it so divergence is a visible bug. The anchor is vessel-independent — multiple vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined package.
60	... {rbbc_require}
61	copy succeeds -- the anchor tag is now a permanent, content-addressed reference in
62	{gar_service}
63	containing all upstream platforms
64	... Single-platform upstream images are handled identically -- `skopeo copy --all` is a no-op on the platform dimension when only one platform exists
65	.. Writes JSON anchor results to `/builder/outputs/output` (returned as `buildStepOutputs`)
66	
67	//axhos_step
68	. *Extract Anchors and Write Regime*:
69	.. Read `buildStepOutputs[0]` from the completed build result (base64-decoded JSON)
70	.. For each slot present in the result:
71	... Write anchor string to `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` in the vessel's `rbrv.env`
72	
73	//axhoot_typed_output
74	{rbrv_image_anchor}
75	Per-slot anchor string written to the vessel regime.
76	
77	//axhoc_completion
78	Successful execution produces anchored base image references in
79	{gar_service}
80	(with all upstream platforms preserved) and updates the vessel regime with
81	{rbrv_image_anchor}
82	values.
83	The vessel is now ready for reproducible
84	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
85	builds using GAR-mirrored base images.
86	Multi-platform conjure vessels pull the correct platform from the anchored manifest list automatically.
87	Re-running enshrine with the same
88	{rbrv_image_origin}
89	but a different upstream digest produces a new anchor (upstream image was updated).
90	Re-running with the same digest is idempotent.
91

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation conjures an
5	{rbtga_ark_image}
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	by building a container image from a vessel specification. The
9	{rbtr_director}
10	performs the build via
11	{gcb_service}
12	using direct `builds.create` API submission.
13	
14	NOTE: Conjure produces both the image and about artifacts in a single Cloud Build job submitted via `builds.create`.
15	The about steps (`rbgja01`-`rbgja04`) are embedded via stitch alongside the build steps.
16	The vouch artifact is created by the subsequent {rbtgo_ark_vouch} pipeline stage (always a separate Cloud Build job).
17	
18	NOTE: The
19	{rbtga_hallmark}
20	is minted locally by the
21	{rbtr_director}
22	before submission.
23	The Director knows the complete artifact tag set before the build begins -- naming authority and verification authority are the same entity.
24	
25	NOTE: The conjure build job runs on the worker pool selected by the vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`: tether pool for vessels needing public internet (e.g., `apt-get` in Dockerfile), airgap pool for vessels with all dependencies enshrined in GAR.
26	The subsequent {rbtgo_ark_vouch} always runs on the airgap pool regardless of vessel egress mode.
27	
28	The conjure operation:
29	
30	//axhopt_typed_parameter
31	{rbtga_ark}
32	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
33	.. {rbbc_require}
34	vessel provided as first argument
35	.. {rbbc_require}
36	vessel contains conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Registry Preflight*:
40	.. *Reliquary check*: HEAD request to `«RELIQUARY»/docker:latest` manifest in
41	{gar_service}.
42	{rbbc_require}
43	HTTP 200 — on 404, fail with directive to run inscribe first.
44	The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images inscribed from upstream into private GAR. Each vessel declares its reliquary; multiple datestamped reliquaries can coexist.
45	.. *Enshrine check*: For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where both
46	{rbrv_image_origin}
47	and
48	{rbrv_image_anchor}
49	are set:
50	... HEAD request to `«PREFIX»enshrines/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` manifest in
51	{gar_service}
52	... {rbbc_require}
53	HTTP 200 — on 404, fail with directive to run {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} first.
54	Multiple vessels sharing the same anchor need only one enshrine.
55	.. Preflight prevents wasted
56	{gcb_service}
57	submission time on missing images, with copy-paste remediation commands
58	
59	//axhos_step
60	. *Resolve Base Images*:
61	.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
62	{rbrv_image_origin}
63	is set:
64	... If
65	{rbrv_image_anchor}
66	is present: resolve to full GAR image reference using the anchor tag
67	... If
68	{rbrv_image_anchor}
69	is absent: pass
70	{rbrv_image_origin}
71	through as the upstream tag (development path)
72	.. Resolved references become `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args for the Dockerfile `ARG`/`FROM` pattern
73	
74	//axhos_step
75	. *Assign Hallmark*:
76	.. Mint
77	{rbtga_hallmark}
78	locally on the Director's workstation
79	.. {rbbc_store}
80	`«HALLMARK»` -- complete artifact tag set is now known before build submission
81	
82	//axhos_step
83	. *Push Pouch*:
84	.. Package build context (Dockerfile and context files) as a FROM SCRATCH OCI image
85	.. Push to GAR tagged `«PREFIX»hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/pouch:«HALLMARK»`
86	.. {rbbc_require}
87	push succeeds -- the pouch delivers build context to
88	{gcb_service}
89	
90	//axhos_step
91	. *Execute Build*:
92	.. Stitch
93	{rbtgr_build_json}
94	with all step image references and substitution variables
95	.. {rbbc_submit}
96	`POST «GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL»` with Build resource JSON via `builds.create`
97	.. All build parameters in stitched
98	{rbtgr_build_json}
99	-- zero custom substitution overrides
100	.. Waits for build completion
101	.. On success, the
102	{rbtga_ark_image}
103	artifact is created with the
104	{rbtga_hallmark}
105	.. {rbtgr_provenance}
106	is auto-generated by Google as a signed attestation on the built image
107	
108	//axhoot_typed_output
109	{rbtga_ark_image}
110	
111	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
112	{rbf_fact_hallmark}
113	The full
114	{rbtga_hallmark}
115	string.
116	
117	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJE Step 01: Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via skopeo
3	# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGE_GAR_HOST, _RBGE_GAR_PATH, _RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN
6	#
7	# For each non-empty ORIGIN slot: inspect upstream manifest, compute anchor,
8	# skopeo copy --all to GAR. Write JSON anchor results to /builder/outputs/output.
9	# Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server for GAR destination.
10	#
11	# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<ENSHRINES_ROOT>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
12	# Anchor as both path segment and tag — each anchor is a peer package under
13	# the enshrines namespace, not a tag on a shared package.

16	echo "=== Enshrine base images to GAR ==="
17

21	  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
22	  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }
23	
24	TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
25	test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
26	
27	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
28	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
29	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
30	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
31	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
32	
33	# Initialize result JSON
34	RESULT='{'
35	FIRST=true
36	
37	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
38	  case "${SLOT}" in
39	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}" ;;
40	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}" ;;
41	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}" ;;
42	  esac
43	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
44	
45	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} ---"
46	
47	  # Inspect upstream for raw manifest (manifest list or single manifest)
48	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/enshrine_raw_${SLOT}.json"
49	  skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
50	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
51	
52	  # Compute sha256 of the raw manifest
53	  # Note: sha256sum (coreutils), not openssl — this runs inside the skopeo reliquary container
54	  SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
55	  test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
56	
57	  # Construct anchor: sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars
58	  SANITIZED=$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')
59	  SHORT="${SHA:0:10}"
60	  ANCHOR="${SANITIZED}-${SHORT}"
61	
62	  echo "Anchor: ${ANCHOR}"
63	  echo "Digest: sha256:${SHA}"
64	
65	  # Construct GAR destination — anchor is both the package name and the tag
66	  DEST_REF="${_RBGE_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGE_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"
67	  echo "Dest: ${DEST_REF}"
68	
69	  # Copy upstream to GAR with anchor tag, preserving manifest list
70	  skopeo copy --all \
71	    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
72	    "docker://${DEST_REF}" \
73	    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
74	    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
75	
76	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} enshrined: ${ANCHOR}"
77

83	  fi
84	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"anchor\":\"${ANCHOR}\",\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\",\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\"}"
85	done
86	
87	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
88

91	
92	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJE Step 01: Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via skopeo
# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGE_GAR_HOST, _RBGE_GAR_PATH, _RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT,
#                _RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN
#
# For each non-empty ORIGIN slot: inspect upstream manifest, compute anchor,
# skopeo copy --all to GAR. Write JSON anchor results to /builder/outputs/output.
# Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server for GAR destination.
#
# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<ENSHRINES_ROOT>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
# Anchor as both path segment and tag — each anchor is a peer package under
# the enshrines namespace, not a tag on a shared package.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Enshrine base images to GAR ==="

# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA)
echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }

# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"

# Initialize result JSON
RESULT='{'
FIRST=true

for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
  case "${SLOT}" in
    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}" ;;
    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}" ;;
    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue

  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} ---"

  # Inspect upstream for raw manifest (manifest list or single manifest)
  RAW_FILE="/workspace/enshrine_raw_${SLOT}.json"
  skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Compute sha256 of the raw manifest
  # Note: sha256sum (coreutils), not openssl — this runs inside the skopeo reliquary container
  SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
  test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Construct anchor: sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars
  SANITIZED=$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')
  SHORT="${SHA:0:10}"
  ANCHOR="${SANITIZED}-${SHORT}"

  echo "Anchor: ${ANCHOR}"
  echo "Digest: sha256:${SHA}"

  # Construct GAR destination — anchor is both the package name and the tag
  DEST_REF="${_RBGE_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGE_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"
  echo "Dest: ${DEST_REF}"

  # Copy upstream to GAR with anchor tag, preserving manifest list
  skopeo copy --all \
    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
    "docker://${DEST_REF}" \
    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "Slot ${SLOT} enshrined: ${ANCHOR}"

  # Accumulate JSON result (no jq dependency — printf safe for sanitized anchor values)
  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
    FIRST=false
  else
    RESULT="${RESULT},"
  fi
  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"anchor\":\"${ANCHOR}\",\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\",\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\"}"
done

RESULT="${RESULT}}"

echo "=== Writing anchor results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Enshrine step complete ==="

#
# A missing image at either layer causes a Cloud Build failure minutes later.
# This preflight catches it immediately with copy-paste remediation commands.
#
# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR)
# and authentication (needs token for registry API).
zrbfd_registry_preflight() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: token required"
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: vessel_dir required"

  # --- Layer 1: Reliquary tool images ---
  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # --- Layer 2: Enshrined base images ---
  # Each vessel declares base images via RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN (upstream tag) and
  # RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR (content-addressed GAR tag). Enshrine copies the upstream
  # image into the enshrine namespace, pinned by the anchor hash. The conjure
  # Dockerfile's FROM references the anchor, not the upstream tag.

  buc_step "Verifying enshrined base images exist in GAR"

  local z_n=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  local z_anchor=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  local z_origin=""
  local z_any_checked="false"
  local z_status_file=""
  local z_response_file=""
  local z_stderr_file=""
  local z_http_code=""

  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"

    # Skip slots without an origin or without an anchor (pass-through images don't need enshrine)
    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue
    test -n "${z_anchor}" || continue

    z_any_checked="true"
    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_status.txt"
    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_response.txt"
    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_stderr.txt"

    curl --head -sS \
      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
      -w "%{http_code}" \
      -o "${z_response_file}" \
      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${z_anchor}/manifests/${z_anchor}" \
      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for enshrined image: ${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${z_anchor}:${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"

    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for enshrine check"

    if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
      buc_warn "Enshrined base image not found: ${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${z_anchor}:${z_anchor} (from ${z_origin})"
      buc_bare "  Enshrine copies upstream base images (e.g., busybox:latest from Docker Hub) into"
      buc_bare "  your private GAR, pinned by content hash. Like the reliquary, this ensures"
      buc_bare "  air-gapped builds never reach the public internet. The anchor tag is stable"
      buc_bare "  until you deliberately re-enshrine to pick up a newer upstream version."
      buc_bare "  Multiple vessels sharing the same base image anchor need only one enshrine."
      buc_bare "  Run enshrine, then re-run ordain:"
      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
      buc_die "Registry preflight failed — enshrined base image missing from GAR"
    elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
      buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking enshrined image: ${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${z_anchor}:${z_anchor}"
    fi

    buc_log_args "Enshrined image verified: ${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${z_anchor}:${z_anchor}"
  done

  if test "${z_any_checked}" = "true"; then
    buc_info "All enshrined base images verified in GAR"
  fi
}


zrbfd_stitch_build_json() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_output_path="${1:?Output path required}"
  local -r z_hallmark="${2:?Hallmark required}"
  local -r z_context_tag="${3:?Context image tag required}"

  buc_log_args "Stitching builds.create JSON to ${z_output_path}"

  # Preconditions: vessel loaded and git state captured
  test -s "${ZRBFC_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE}" || buc_die "Vessel not loaded — call zrbfc_load_vessel first"
  test -s "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}"     || buc_die "Git info not captured — ensure git metadata is captured before stitch"

  buc_log_args 'Read vessel state for substitutions'
  local -r z_sigil=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_sigil}" || buc_die "Empty vessel sigil"
  local -r z_dockerfile_name="${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE##*/}"
  local -r z_platforms="${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,}"

  # Resolve base images: ANCHOR → full GAR reference, or pass ORIGIN through
  # Spec: RBSAC step "Resolve Base Images"
  # New layout: each anchor is its own package under RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT, with anchor as both path segment and tag.
  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local z_image_ref_1="" z_image_ref_2="" z_image_ref_3=""
  local z_ri_n="" z_ri_origin_var="" z_ri_anchor_var="" z_ri_origin="" z_ri_anchor=""
  for z_ri_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_ri_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_ri_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_ri_origin="${!z_ri_origin_var:-}"
    z_ri_anchor="${!z_ri_anchor_var:-}"
    test -n "${z_ri_origin}" || continue

393	      buc_log_args "Image slot ${z_ri_n} (anchored): ${z_ri_ref}"
394	    else

399	      1) z_image_ref_1="${z_ri_ref}" ;; 2) z_image_ref_2="${z_ri_ref}" ;; 3) z_image_ref_3="${z_ri_ref}" ;;
400	    esac
401	  done
402	
403	  # Platform count detection
404	  local z_platform_count=0
405	  local z_remaining_count="${z_platforms}"
406	  local z_p_count=""
407	  while test -n "${z_remaining_count}"; do
408	    z_p_count="${z_remaining_count%%,*}"
409	    z_platform_count=$((z_platform_count + 1))
410	    test "${z_remaining_count}" != "${z_p_count}" || break
411	    z_remaining_count="${z_remaining_count#*,}"
412	  done
413	  buc_log_args "Vessel platforms: ${z_platform_count} (${z_platforms})"
414	
415	  buc_log_args 'Extract git state for substitutions'
416	  local -r z_stitch_git_commit_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}git_commit.txt"
417	  local -r z_stitch_git_branch_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}git_branch.txt"
418	  local -r z_stitch_git_repo_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}git_repo.txt"
419	
420	  jq -r '.commit' "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_commit_file}" \
421	    || buc_die "Failed to extract git commit from info file"
422	  jq -r '.branch' "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_branch_file}" \
423	    || buc_die "Failed to extract git branch from info file"
424	  jq -r '.repo'   "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_repo_file}" \
425	    || buc_die "Failed to extract git repo from info file"
426	
427	  local -r z_git_commit=$(<"${z_stitch_git_commit_file}")
428	  local -r z_git_branch=$(<"${z_stitch_git_branch_file}")
429	  local -r z_git_repo=$(<"${z_stitch_git_repo_file}")
430	
431	  test -n "${z_git_commit}" || buc_die "Git commit is empty"
432	  test -n "${z_git_branch}" || buc_die "Git branch is empty"
433	  test -n "${z_git_repo}"   || buc_die "Git repo is empty"

test -n "${z_ri_origin}" || continue
    local z_ri_ref=""
    if test -n "${z_ri_anchor}"; then
      z_ri_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${z_ri_anchor}:${z_ri_anchor}"
      buc_log_args "Image slot ${z_ri_n} (anchored): ${z_ri_ref}"
    else
      z_ri_ref="${z_ri_origin}"
      buc_log_args "Image slot ${z_ri_n} (pass-through): ${z_ri_ref}"
    fi
    case "${z_ri_n}" in
      1) z_image_ref_1="${z_ri_ref}" ;; 2) z_image_ref_2="${z_ri_ref}" ;; 3) z_image_ref_3="${z_ri_ref}" ;;
    esac
  done

  # Platform count detection
  local z_platform_count=0
  local z_remaining_count="${z_platforms}"
  local z_p_count=""
  while test -n "${z_remaining_count}"; do
    z_p_count="${z_remaining_count%%,*}"
    z_platform_count=$((z_platform_count + 1))
    test "${z_remaining_count}" != "${z_p_count}" || break
    z_remaining_count="${z_remaining_count#*,}"
  done
  buc_log_args "Vessel platforms: ${z_platform_count} (${z_platforms})"

  buc_log_args 'Extract git state for substitutions'
  local -r z_stitch_git_commit_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}git_commit.txt"
  local -r z_stitch_git_branch_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}git_branch.txt"
  local -r z_stitch_git_repo_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}git_repo.txt"

  jq -r '.commit' "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_commit_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract git commit from info file"
  jq -r '.branch' "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_branch_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract git branch from info file"
  jq -r '.repo'   "${ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE}" > "${z_stitch_git_repo_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to extract git repo from info file"

  local -r z_git_commit=$(<"${z_stitch_git_commit_file}")
  local -r z_git_branch=$(<"${z_stitch_git_branch_file}")
  local -r z_git_repo=$(<"${z_stitch_git_repo_file}")

  test -n "${z_git_commit}" || buc_die "Git commit is empty"
  test -n "${z_git_branch}" || buc_die "Git branch is empty"
  test -n "${z_git_repo}"   || buc_die "Git repo is empty"

  # Build strategy: compare vessel platforms against runner platform
  # If platforms exactly match the runner, no QEMU emulation is needed (native build).
  # Any difference (multi-platform or non-native single-platform) requires binfmt.
  local z_needs_binfmt="true"
  if test "${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,}" = "${RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM}"; then
    z_needs_binfmt="false"
  fi

  local z_build_strategy=""
  if test "${z_needs_binfmt}" = "true"; then
    z_build_strategy="emulated multi-platform via QEMU (${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS// /,})"
    buc_log_args "Build strategy: ${z_build_strategy} — rbgjb02 included"
  else
    z_build_strategy="native single-platform (${RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM})"
    buc_log_args "Build strategy: ${z_build_strategy} — rbgjb02 excluded"
  fi

  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
  # Entrypoint 'bash' → #!/bin/bash shebang, 'sh' → #!/bin/sh shebang (GCB script field)
  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
  # Pipeline: buildx --push → per-platform pullback → SLSA provenance via images: field
  local z_step_defs=(
    "rbgjb01-derive-tag-base.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|bash|derive-tag-base"
  )
  if test "${z_needs_binfmt}" = "true"; then
    z_step_defs+=("rbgjb02-qemu-binfmt.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|qemu-binfmt")
  fi
  z_step_defs+=(
    "rbgjb03-buildx-push-image.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|buildx-push-image"
    "rbgjb04-per-platform-pullback.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|per-platform-pullback"
    "rbgjb05-push-per-platform.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|push-per-platform"
    "rbgjb06-push-diags.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|push-diags"
  )

file.content:
# buildStepOutputs[0] is base64-encoded JSON from the enshrine step
  local -r z_b64_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}output_b64.txt"
  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}output.json"

  jq -r '.results.buildStepOutputs[0] // empty' "${ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE}" \
    > "${z_b64_file}" || buc_die "Failed to extract buildStepOutputs from build result"
  test -s "${z_b64_file}" || buc_die "No buildStepOutputs in build result — enshrine step did not produce output"

  openssl enc -base64 -d -A < "${z_b64_file}" > "${z_output_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to decode buildStepOutputs base64"
  test -s "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Empty decoded buildStepOutputs"

  buc_log_args "Enshrine output:"
  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"

  # Write each anchor back to the vessel regime file
  local z_n=""
  local z_slot_key=""
  local z_anchor=""
  local z_anchor_var=""
  local z_anchor_line=""
  local z_updated_file=""

  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_slot_key="slot_${z_n}"
    z_anchor=$(jq -r ".${z_slot_key}.anchor // empty" "${z_output_file}") || z_anchor=""
    test -n "${z_anchor}" || continue

    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_anchor_line="${z_anchor_var}=${z_anchor}"
    z_updated_file="${ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX}${z_n}_updated_rbrv.env"

    buc_step "Writing anchor slot ${z_n}: ${z_anchor}"

    # Replace existing ANCHOR line or append — bash-native read/match/write
    local z_found=false
    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
      if [[ "${z_line}" == ${z_anchor_var}=* ]]; then
        printf '%s\n' "${z_anchor_line}"
        z_found=true
      else
        printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"
      fi
    done < "${z_rbrv_file}" > "${z_updated_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to process ${z_rbrv_file} for ${z_anchor_var}"

    if [[ "${z_found}" != "true" ]]; then
      printf '%s\n' "${z_anchor_line}" >> "${z_updated_file}" \
        || buc_die "Failed to append ${z_anchor_var}"
    fi

    cp "${z_updated_file}" "${z_rbrv_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write updated rbrv.env"

    buc_success "Slot ${z_n} enshrined: ${z_anchor}"
  done
}

rbfd_ordain() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ordain a hallmark from a vessel (conjure, mirror, or graft based on vessel mode)"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path)
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"

  # Peek at vessel mode without sourcing (sourcing makes vars readonly,
  # and the downstream function will source again via zrbfc_load_vessel)
  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
  local z_mode=""
  local z_mode_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_mode_line || test -n "${z_mode_line}"; do
    case "${z_mode_line}" in
      RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=*) z_mode="${z_mode_line#RBRV_VESSEL_MODE=}"; break ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrv_file}"

fi

  # === Assemble about steps ===
  local -r z_about_steps_file="${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}about_steps.json"
  zrbfc_assemble_about_steps "${z_about_steps_file}" "${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}about_"

  # === Resolve base image provenance (for vouch summary) ===
  # New layout: anchored base images live at <ENSHRINES_ROOT>/<anchor>:<anchor>
  local -r z_vi_gar_repo_base="${z_gar_host}/${z_gar_path}"
  local z_vi_ref_1="" z_vi_ref_2="" z_vi_ref_3=""
  local z_vi_prov_1="" z_vi_prov_2="" z_vi_prov_3=""
  local z_vi_n="" z_vi_origin_var="" z_vi_anchor_var="" z_vi_origin="" z_vi_anchor=""
  for z_vi_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_vi_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_vi_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_vi_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_vi_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_vi_origin="${!z_vi_origin_var:-}"
    z_vi_anchor="${!z_vi_anchor_var:-}"
    test -n "${z_vi_origin}" || continue
    local z_vi_ref="" z_vi_prov=""
    if test -n "${z_vi_anchor}"; then
      z_vi_ref="${z_vi_gar_repo_base}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${z_vi_anchor}:${z_vi_anchor}"
      z_vi_prov="anchored"
    else
      z_vi_ref="${z_vi_origin}"
      z_vi_prov="pass-through"
    fi
    case "${z_vi_n}" in
      1) z_vi_ref_1="${z_vi_ref}"; z_vi_prov_1="${z_vi_prov}" ;;
      2) z_vi_ref_2="${z_vi_ref}"; z_vi_prov_2="${z_vi_prov}" ;;
      3) z_vi_ref_3="${z_vi_ref}"; z_vi_prov_3="${z_vi_prov}" ;;
    esac
  done

  # === Assemble vouch steps ===
  local -r z_vouch_steps_file="${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}vouch_steps.json"
  zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps "${z_vouch_steps_file}" "${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}vouch_"

  # === Combine: about steps + vouch steps ===
  local -r z_combined_steps="${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}combined_steps.json"
  jq -s '.[0] + .[1]' "${z_about_steps_file}" "${z_vouch_steps_file}" \
    > "${z_combined_steps}" || buc_die "Failed to combine about and vouch steps"

  # Compose Build resource JSON with both _RBGA_ and _RBGV_ substitutions
  buc_log_args "Composing combined about+vouch Build resource JSON"
  local -r z_build_file="${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}build.json"

  jq -n \
    --slurpfile zjq_steps       "${z_combined_steps}" \
    --arg zjq_sa                "${z_mason_sa}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_host          "${z_gar_host}" \
